query_normalized: naturebench: can coding agents match the published sota of nature-family papers? linguistics
rationale: The paper focuses on morphological processing of a specific low-resource language (Tangkhul) using unsupervised methods, which does not address the user's question about evaluating the performance of coding agents against SOTA in scientific (Nature-family) papers. There is no overlap in the mechanism (coding agents vs. morphological analysis), domain (software engineering/LLM benchmarks vs. linguistics corpus creation), or variables being measured.
